# Geospatial Visualization

## Introduction 



## Graph 



```{r, message = F, echo = F, warning = F}
# load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(colorspace)
library(here)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(urbnmapr)
library(usmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

# more efficient to generate sf maps
if (!identical(getOption("bitmapType"), "cairo") && isTRUE(capabilities()[["cairo"]])) {
  options(bitmapType = "cairo")
}

# set default theme for ggplot2
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 16))
# set default figure parameters for knitr
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.width = 8, fig.asp = 0.618,
  fig.retina = 2, dpi = 150
)

#Loading SEDA data
test_dist <- read_csv(file = here("Data", "seda_geodist_long_gcs_5.0_updated_20240319.csv"))
cov_dist <- read_csv(file = here("Data", "seda_cov_geodist_pool_5.0_updated_20240319.csv"))
crosswalk <- read_csv(file = here("Data", "seda_crosswalk_5.0_updated_20240319.csv"))

#Merging covariate and achievement data 
test_cov_dist <- left_join(x = test_dist, y = cov_dist, by = c("sedalea", "stateabb"))

#Covariate data with percent of households below the poverty line

#Average poverty by state
cov_state <- test_cov_dist %>%
    group_by(stateabb) %>%
    summarize(pov_per = mean(povertyavgall, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") 

#Using quantiles for high, low, and average 
p25 <- quantile(cov_state$pov_per, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
p75 <- quantile(cov_state$pov_per, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

#Adding quantiles to data
cov_state <-  cov_state %>%
    mutate(pov_state = case_when(pov_per <= p25 ~ "Low poverty",
                                 pov_per >= p75 ~ "High poverty",
                                  TRUE ~ "Average poverty"),
            pov_state = factor(pov_state, levels = c("Low poverty", "Average poverty", "High poverty")))


#Average math test data to be district level 
test_long <- test_dist %>%
  group_by(sedalea, as.factor(subject)) %>%
  summarise(score = mean(gcs_mn_all, na.rm = TRUE), 
            stateabb = unique(stateabb),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from  = 'as.factor(subject)', values_from = score) 

#Changing district math score to be centered at the median 
test_long <- test_long %>%
  mutate(mth_bin = cut(mth, breaks = quantile(mth, probs = c(0, .05, .35, .65, .95, 1),na.rm = TRUE),
                       labels = c("Bottom 5%","Below average","Average","Above average","Top 5%"),
                       include.lowest = TRUE))

#Merging test and poverty data
test_long <- left_join(x = test_long, y = cov_state, by = "stateabb")

#Creating crosswalk on the district level
district_id <- crosswalk %>%
    distinct(sedalea, leaid, sedaleaname)

#Merging SEDA datasets with district IDs
test_cov_id <- left_join(x = test_long, y = district_id, by = c("sedalea"))

#Merging Spatial data
dist <- st_read(dsn = here("Data", "EDGE_SCHOOLDISTRICT_TL24_SY2324.shp"))

# make SEDA leaid a 7-digit string
test_cov_id2 <- test_cov_id %>%
  mutate(leaid = str_pad(as.character(leaid), 7, pad = "0"))

# join using GEOID
test_dist_all <- dist %>%
  left_join(test_cov_id2, by = c("GEOID" = "leaid"))

# Get urbnmapr states
states_sf <- get_urbn_map("states", sf = TRUE)
sf_use_s2(FALSE) # not using the s2 spherical geometry package to be able to move Hawaii and Alaska

#Inset transform districts
districts_inset <- test_dist_all %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>% # coordinates are latitude and longitude.
  st_make_valid() %>% # fix invalid geometries
  st_buffer(0) %>%  # rebuild boundaries
  st_simplify(dTolerance = 0.0005, preserveTopology = TRUE) %>%  # Remove vertices that are super close together
  usmap_transform() #Transform so that Alaska and Hawaii are on the bottom

#Inset transform states 
states_inset <- states_sf %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  usmap_transform()

#Creating an appropriate box for the map
bb <- st_bbox(states_inset)

districts_inset2 <- st_crop(districts_inset, bb)

# Including AK and HI
states_outline50 <- states_inset %>%
  st_crop(bb)

# Drop AK + HI from the outline layer only because it wasn't mapping correctly
states_outline48 <- states_inset %>%
 filter(!state_abbv %in% c("AK", "HI")) %>%
  st_crop(bb)

#Add poverty data to states
states_outline50_pov <- left_join(x = states_outline50, y = cov_state, by = c("state_abbv" = "stateabb"))

# Creating points for poverty instead
state_pov <- states_outline50_pov %>%
  filter(!is.na(pov_state))

#Fixing color palette
cols <- brewer.pal(5, "RdYlBu")
cols[3] <- "#FEE08B"

#Plot
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = districts_inset2, aes(fill = mth_bin), color = NA) +
 geom_sf(data = subset(state_pov, pov_state == "High poverty"),fill = NA,color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_sf(data = states_outline48, fill = NA, color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.1) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(bb["xmin"], bb["xmax"]),
    ylim = c(bb["ymin"], bb["ymax"]),
    expand = FALSE, datum = NA) +
     scale_fill_manual("Math Performance",
                       values = cols,
                       na.value = "grey90",
                       breaks = c("Bottom 5%","Below average","Average","Above average","Top 5%")) +
  labs(title = "Average Math Achievement by School District",
       subtitle = "School district math achievement (binned relative to national average)",
       caption = "Source: Stanford Educational Opportunity Project (SEDA v5.0) and NCES EDGE district boundaries (SY 2023–24).") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
             legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
             plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
             plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
             plot.caption = element_text(size = 7))
```

## Insights

From homework 3
